action.hypothesis: Returning to the best known working JumpReLU K=128 configuration and modestly increasing AUXK_ALPHA to 0.0625 may improve training stability and feature health without changing the core architecture or optimization regime, potentially recovering some proxy quality after the recent stalled sweeps.
action.summary: Use the current best working JumpReLU K=128 setup as the stability anchor and make one minor parameter adjustment by testing AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 in proxy.
action.self_review: This respects crash-recovery constraints by avoiding code edits and new architectures, and it returns to the strongest validated configuration with a single conservative parameter change. The main risk is that higher auxk may over-regularize reconstruction, but it is a safer stability-oriented probe than another architecture shift.
notes_to_memory: Crash-recovery mode requires returning to the best known working configuration and limiting the round to a minor parameter-only adjustment.
This round restores the strongest JumpReLU K=128 baseline and tests a slightly stronger auxiliary dead-feature objective for a stability-oriented probe.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 does not help, the remaining conservative fallback is an LR reduction on the same baseline rather than another architecture change.
next_hypotheses: If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and treat the higher-auxk regime as the new stable JumpReLU anchor.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 is worse but training remains healthy, test LR=2e-4 on the same restored baseline as the next stability-oriented adjustment.
If stability problems persist even on the restored baseline, pause architecture exploration and continue only with conservative recovery steps around the known-good JumpReLU configuration.
